points.text: VERAXA Biotech AG is advancing its lead BiTAC®-TCE program, a bispecific T cell engager format, by initiating cell line development with ATUM to accelerate clinical development.
VERAXA Biotech AG expanded its R&D hub in Heidelberg, Germany, with a greater than 60% increase in laboratory space to support its bispecific T cell engager and bispecific ADC programs.

Genmab announced positive Phase 3 results for epcoritamab (a bispecific antibody) plus lenalidomide in relapsed/refractory diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, demonstrating statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival.

Xilio Therapeutics — XTX501 (bispecific PD-1 / masked IL-2) — Solid tumors — On track for IND submission mid-2026 and Phase 1 initiation H2 2026; initial data expected H2 2027 in metastatic non-small cell lung cancer.

NovaBridge Biosciences received FDA Fast Track Designation for givastomig, a CLDN18.2 x 4-1BB bispecific antibody, in combination with nivolumab and chemotherapy for first-line HER2-negative metastatic gastric cancer.
VERAXA Biotech — BiTAC®-TCE — Cancer — Initiated cell line development with ATUM to advance lead bispecific T cell engager program toward clinical development.
VERAXA Biotech — BiTAC platform (bispecific TCE and ADC) — Oncology — Expanded Heidelberg R&D facility by >60% to accommodate team additions and infrastructure for bispecific drug development.

Genmab — Epcoritamab + Lenalidomide — Relapsed/Refractory Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma — Phase 3 trial met primary endpoint with statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival; epcoritamab is a CD20xCD3 bispecific antibody.
Xilio Therapeutics — XTX501 — Solid tumors — Bispecific PD-1/masked IL-2 program advancing to IND-enabling studies; planned Phase 1 initiation in H2 2026.

NovaBridge Biosciences — Givastomig — First-line HER2-negative metastatic gastric cancer — FDA granted Fast Track Designation for CLDN18.2 x 4-1BB bispecific antibody in combination with nivolumab and chemotherapy; Phase 3 trial expected to begin Q4 2026.

VERAXA Biotech — Next-generation antibody therapeutics commercialization — BiTAC bispecific T cell engagers and bispecific ADCs — R&D hub expanded in Heidelberg; cell line development initiated for lead candidate.

Genmab — Bispecific antibody commercialization and pipeline expansion — Epcoritamab (CD20xCD3 bispecific) — Phase 3 positive data in DLBCL; company maintains broad portfolio including ADCs and immune-modulating antibodies.

Xilio Therapeutics — Masked immuno-oncology bispecific development — XTX501 (bispecific PD-1/masked IL-2) — IND submission planned mid-2026; achieved financial milestone under AbbVie collaboration extending cash runway to early 2028.

NovaBridge Biosciences — Accelerating CLDN18.2-directed bispecific therapy — Givastomig (TJ033721) — Phase 1b data supports accelerated approval pathway; registrational Phase 3 trial initiation planned for Q4 2026.

BiTAC®-TCE — Mechanism: Bispecific T cell engager utilizing two complementary precursors for tumor-restricted activation — Phase: Pre-clinical/IND-enabling — Population: Cancer patients — Status: Cell line development initiated with ATUM to support clinical entry.

Epcoritamab — Mechanism: CD20xCD3 bispecific T-cell engager — Phase: 3 — Population: Relapsed/refractory diffuse large B-cell lymphoma — Comparator: Lenalidomide combination — Endpoints/Results: Statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival reported.

XTX501 — Mechanism: Bispecific PD-1 / masked IL-2 designed to selectively stimulate PD-1 positive antigen-experienced T cells while overcoming IL-2 receptor-mediated clearance — Phase: Preclinical/IND-enabling — Population: Solid tumors (metastatic NSCLC planned) — Comparator: None stated — Results: Robust monotherapy activity in PD-1 insensitive settings; tumor-selective pharmacodynamics confirmed.

Givastomig — Mechanism: CLDN18.2 x 4-1BB bispecific antibody — Phase: 1b (data presented) — Population: HER2-negative advanced/metastatic gastroesophageal adenocarcinomas (CLDN18.2+ and PD-L1+) — Comparator: Historical benchmarks for standard of care — Results: Deep and durable responses with marked improvement vs historical benchmarks; favorable tolerability in combination with immunochemotherapy.
VERAXA Biotech — Strategic R&D expansion — BiTAC bispecific pipeline — Secured long-term lease for >60% larger laboratory space in Heidelberg, Germany, to support bispecific TCE and ADC development.
Xilio Therapeutics — AbbVie collaboration milestone — XTX501 and masked T-cell engager programs — Achieved development milestone extending cash runway into early 2028.
